subject: Mathematics
question_text: Sum of two supplementary angles is 180°.
answer_explanation: Acute angles are those angles which are less than 90°. If we add two angles which are less than 90°, we get the result less than 180°, e.g. If we add 60° and 70°, we get 60° + 70° = 130° < 180°. Hence, two acute angles cannot form a pair of supplementary angles. shaalaa.com [
provenance_excerpt: Sum of two supplementary angles is 180°.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If sum of measures of two angles is 90°, then the angles are _________.
answer_explanation: Let one angle be 2x and the other angle be 2x + 2. We know that the sum of the measures of the complementary angles is 90°. According to the question, 2x + 2x + 2 = 90° ⇒ 4x = 90° – 2 = 88° ⇒ x = 88∘4 = 22° ∴ 2x = 2 × 22° = 44° and 2x + 2 = 44° + 2 = 46° Thus, one angle is 44° and other is 46°. shaalaa.com     Chapter 5: Lines and Angles - Exercise  Chapter 5 Lines and Angles   Exercise | Q 95. | Page 145 ####  Video TutorialsVIEW ALL  ####

question_text: A transversal intersects two or more than two lines at _________ points. If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, then (Q. 45 to 48).
answer_explanation: Let one angle be x. It is given that other angle is one-third of first. So, other angle will be 13⁢𝑥. Again, given that both the angles are making a linear pair. So, their sum will be 180°. ∴ 𝑥+13⁢𝑥=180∘ ⇒ 3⁢𝑥+𝑥3=180∘ ...[Taking LCM of 1 and 3 on LHS] ⇒ 4⁢𝑥3=180∘ ⇒ 𝑥=180∘×34 ⇒ x = 135° Hence, the angles are 135° and 13×135∘, i.e. 135° and 45°. shaalaa.com [

question_text: sum of interior angles on the same side of a transversal is                .
answer_explanation: Let one angle be 2x + 1, then the other angle is 2x + 3. We know that the sum of the measures of the supplementary angles is 180°. According to the question, 2x + 1 + 2x + 3 = 180° ⇒ 4x + 4 = 180° ⇒ 4x = 180° – 4 = 176° ⇒ x = 176∘4 = 44° ∴ 2x + 1 = 2 × 44° + 1 = 88° + 1 = 89° And 2x + 3 = 2 × 44° + 3 = 88° + 3 = 91° Thus, one angle is 89° and other is 91°. shaalaa.com [
